source_pr.title: lightning: fix flaky integration test
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #65890

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?
the target log line might appear quite above in the log file, in the failed case it's the 52th line from the bottom. change the tail input to 100
### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: The `lightning_error_summary` test script was flaky because it only inspected the last 50 log lines; the expected message could appear slightly earlier depending on log volume, so the check could miss it intermittently.
failure_signature: lightning_error_summary: expected log line not found in tail output because tail window was too small
root_cause_explanation: The test asserted expected messages appear near the end of a log file but only tailed 50 lines; on failure the target line could be further above, so the grep/check would miss it. This PR increases the tail window to 100 lines, making the log check robust to log-volume variance.
fix_pattern: Make log-based assertions robust: widen the tail window (or search the full log) so checks don't depend on messages being within an arbitrary last-N-lines cutoff.

evidence: evidence: lightning: fix flaky integration test
